avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh wind slabs represent the main danger. The wind will be strong.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In some localities 10 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall during the night above approximately 2000 m. The wind will be strong. As a consequence of new snow and a strong wind from northerly directions, further wind slabs will form also in areas not adjacent to ridgelines. Along the border with France and along the border with Switzerland these avalanche prone locations are more prevalent.
The wind slabs can be released, caution is to be exercised at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. Whumpfing sounds serve as an alarm sign.
Especially on wind-loaded slopes the dry avalanches can be released in the various wind slab layers and reach medium size.
Backcountry touring calls for meticulous route selection.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some localities 5 to 20 cm of snow has fallen since Sunday above approximately 2000 m.
The wind was strong to storm force over a wide area. The wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly. Over a wide area snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is hard and its surface has a crust that is strong in many cases, in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is soft and has a loosely bonded surface, especially in places that are protected from the wind.
The solar radiation gave rise to settling of the snowpack.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The wind will be strong. The avalanche prone locations are to be found on wind-loaded slopes at elevated altitudes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger. Weakly bonded old snow is to be evaluated with care and prudence.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a strong to storm force wind, extensive wind slabs will form in all aspects. These can be released easily. or in isolated cases naturally, above approximately 2200 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain, as well as adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Mostly avalanches are medium-sized.

Additionally in isolated cases avalanches can penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

Shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack can indicate the danger.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall. The sometimes storm force wind will transport the new snow significantly. In some cases the various wind slabs have bonded poorly together.

The old snowpack will be weakly bonded in some places, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: The avalanche danger will persist. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow represent the main danger.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of new snow and wind the prevalence and size of the avalanche prone locations will increase.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a strong wind from northerly directions, further wind slabs will form in all aspects. The old wind slabs will be covered with new snow in some cases and therefore difficult to recognise. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant.
Weak layers exist in the old snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. Avalanches can be triggered in the old snowpack reach large size in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The small quantity of fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes. Avalanches can in some places be released in deep layers in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to moistening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: New snow and wind slabs in all aspects. Above approximately 1900 m the wind slabs are larger. Wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence.
avalancheActivity.comment: As a consequence of new snow and a strong to storm force northeasterly wind, sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs will form in particular in places that are protected from the wind. In all aspects and above approximately 1800 m more medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large slab avalanches are possible. As a consequence of the snowfall more mostly small moist avalanches are possible below approximately 1800 m.

snowpackStructure.comment: New snow and wind slabs are lying mostly on a moist old snowpack. The wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The wind slabs are easy to recognise and can be released by large loads in particular.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The snowpack will be well bonded at low and intermediate altitudes.

As a consequence of new snow and a moderate to strong wind from easterly directions, clearly visible wind slabs formed in the last two days in particular in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. These can in isolated cases be released by a single winter sport participant.
In some cases the avalanches can be released in the various wind slab layers and reach medium size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some regions 10 cm of snow fell on Sunday above approximately 1800 m.
As a consequence of a moderate to strong wind, clearly visible wind slabs formed in the last few days in particular in areas not adjacent to ridgelines.

Especially slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Individual weak layers exist in the centre of the snowpack.

In particular sunny slopes low and intermediate altitudes: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften later than the day before.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on steep shady slopes at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh and somewhat older wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in some cases at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions, especially at their margins.
The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at transitions into gullies and bowls and on steep shady slopes above approximately 2200 m.
Avalanches can in isolated cases penetrate deep layers and reach medium size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area wind slabs are lying on a hard crust.
In particular adjacent to ridgelines at high altitude snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

On sunnier slopes where temperatures are less cold, the hard surface of the snowpack will gradually soften over the course of the day.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger. Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a storm force northerly wind, extensive wind slabs formed in all aspects. These can be released even by a single winter sport participant above approximately 2200 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain, as well as adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Mostly avalanches are medium-sized.

Additionally in isolated cases avalanches can penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

Shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack can indicate the danger.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Some snow will fall in some regions. The sometimes storm force wind will transport the fresh and old snow. In some cases the various wind slabs have bonded still only poorly together.

The old snowpack will be weakly bonded in some places, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of a sometimes strong wind from northerly directions, further wind slabs will form. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and wet snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In particular on steep slopes individual small to medium-sized slab avalanches are possible above approximately 1400 m.
In the vicinity of peaks a dangerous avalanche situation will be encountered in some localities. The more recent wind slabs and can be released easily.
Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach medium size in some cases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The small quantity of fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
The high temperatures from the late morning will give rise to increasing softening of the snowpack in some cases in particular on sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Moderate avalanche danger will prevail. At elevated altitudes the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent.
avalancheActivity.comment: The fresh and older wind slabs remain in some cases prone to triggering. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The wind slabs can still be released. This applies in particular in case of a large load. Restraint should be exercised because avalanches can sweep people along and give rise to falls.

snowpackStructure.comment: The surface of the snowpack is generally characterised by alternating areas of erosion interspersed with areas of accumulation; in many areas there are hard surface crusts, some of which are hidden. Near the ridges, the wind over the past few days has led to the formation of snow overhanging mass. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be cloudy. The wind will be strong at times.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The avalanche prone locations are to be found on wind-loaded slopes at elevated altitudes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Some snow has fallen since Sunday in some regions. As a consequence of a moderate to strong bise wind, clearly visible wind slabs formed in the last few days.

Today the wind will be strong at times. As a consequence of a strengthening northwesterly wind, mostly shallow wind slabs will form in the course of the day in particular in areas not adjacent to ridgelines. Adjacent to ridgelines at high altitude snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Wind slabs can in some places be released by small loads and reach medium size. This applies especially in areas where the snow cover is rather shallow, and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at intermediate and high altitudes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some regions 5 to 10 cm of snow has fallen since Sunday above approximately 1700 m.
High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: In the vicinity of peaks hardly any snow is lying.
Below the wind-eroded peaks, above around 1600 m, more snow is lying for the time of year.

Over a wide area various wind slab layers are lying on a hard crust.
Especially west, north and east facing slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Individual weak layers exist in the snowpack.

In particular sunny slopes low and intermediate altitudes: The surface of the snowpack will soften later than the day before.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger. Weakly bonded old snow is to be evaluated with care and prudence.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The wind slabs can be released even by a single winter sport participant above the tree line, caution is to be exercised in particular at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain, as well as adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Mostly avalanches are medium-sized.

Additionally in isolated cases avalanches can penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m.

Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack can indicate the danger.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some cases the various wind slabs have bonded still only poorly together.

The old snowpack will be weakly bonded in some places, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow are to be assessed with care and prudence.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh wind slabs represent the main danger. The wind will be strong.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In some localities 0 to 5 cm of snow will fall during the night above approximately 1800 m. The wind will be strong. In some places the wind slabs will increase in size moderately as the day progresses. These can in some places be released by a single winter sport participant, caution is to be exercised at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.
Especially on wind-loaded slopes the dry avalanches can be released in the various wind slab layers and reach medium size.
Backcountry touring calls for meticulous route selection.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some localities 2 to 10 cm of snow, but less in some localities, has fallen since Sunday above approximately 2000 m.
The wind was strong to storm force over a wide area. The wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly. Over a wide area snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is hard and its surface has a crust that is strong in many cases, in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is soft and has a loosely bonded surface, especially in places that are protected from the wind.
The solar radiation gave rise to settling of the snowpack.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The wind will be strong. The avalanche prone locations are to be found on wind-loaded slopes at elevated altitudes.

regions.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.comment: In particular on steep slopes more small avalanches are possible as a consequence of the snowfall.

snowpackStructure.comment: These spring-like weather conditions will give rise to slight settling of the snowpack. This applies in particular on steep sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and wet snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In the vicinity of peaks a generally favourable avalanche situation will be encountered in some localities. The more recent wind slabs are mostly small and can be released by large loads at their margins in particular.
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Above approximately 1500 m small small avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack will be generally stable. The small quantity of fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence.
avalancheActivity.comment: Wind slabs are in some cases prone to triggering in particular on west to north to east facing aspects above the tree line. Avalanches can in very isolated cases penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack reach medium size in isolated cases.

snowpackStructure.comment: The wind will be storm force in some cases. The wind will transport the snow. Avalanche prone wind slabs formed.

tendency.highlights: Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence.
